qa_pairs.question: Who needs to request access to computational resources offered by the Alliance?
qa_pairs.answer: The Principal Investigator (PI) and any sponsored user that needs those resources must first request access.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users request access to the desired computational system(s)?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can request access on this page: https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/me/access_systems.

qa_pairs.question: What might be required before access to some computational systems is granted?
qa_pairs.answer: Some systems will require the acceptance of additional agreements, such as Terms of Use or SLAs, before access can be granted.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if a user misses the step of requesting system access and accepting agreements?
qa_pairs.answer: If this step is missed, users will not be able to log in to the desired system.

qa_pairs.question: When are some storage resources made available in the Default RAP for PIs and sponsored users?
qa_pairs.answer: Some storage resources are made available immediately after creating an Alliance account on CCDB.

qa_pairs.question: What must be done for default storage resources to be ready for utilization?
qa_pairs.answer: Access to the corresponding system must be requested here: https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/me/access_systems.

qa_pairs.question: How can PIs request additional storage resources if the default ones are insufficient?
qa_pairs.answer: PIs can request additional storage resources in any General Purpose cluster without submitting a RAC application.

qa_pairs.question: What is the maximum amount of project storage a PI can request via RAS without a RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: A PI can request up to a maximum of 40 TB of project storage.

qa_pairs.question: What is the maximum amount of nearline storage a PI can request via RAS without a RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: A PI can request up to a maximum of 100 TB of nearline storage.

qa_pairs.question: Can additional RAS storage resources be split across multiple clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, these resources can be requested all in one cluster or split across multiple ones, but the total amount across all clusters must not exceed 40 TB of project storage or 100 TB of nearline storage.

qa_pairs.question: In which project will resources allocated via RAS be available?
qa_pairs.answer: Resources allocated via RAS will be available in the Default Resource Allocation Project (RAP).

qa_pairs.question: Which clusters offer RAS project and nearline storage?
qa_pairs.answer: Fir, Nibi, Rorqual, and Narval offer RAS project and nearline storage.

qa_pairs.question: What are the collective maximum project storage limits for Fir, Nibi, Rorqual, and Narval clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: The maximum project storage across all these clusters is 40 TB.

qa_pairs.question: What are the collective maximum nearline storage limits for Fir, Nibi, Rorqual, and Narval clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: The maximum nearline storage across all these clusters is 100 TB.

qa_pairs.question: Which clusters do not offer RAS storage?
qa_pairs.answer: Trillium and HPSS do not have RAS storage available.

qa_pairs.question: Who can request storage resources via RAS?
qa_pairs.answer: Only PIs (not sponsored users) can request storage resources via RAS.

qa_pairs.question: How do PIs request storage resources via RAS?
qa_pairs.answer: PIs must send an email to support@tech.alliancecan.ca with details of the storage resources needed.

qa_pairs.question: Are CPU resources available for opportunistic use?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, CPU resources are available for 'opportunistic use' to all research groups with an active Alliance account.

qa_pairs.question: How do job priorities compare between opportunistic submissions and RAC allocations?
qa_pairs.answer: The great majority of jobs submitted opportunistically get executed, albeit sometimes with a lower priority than jobs submitted with a RAC allocation.

qa_pairs.question: What is the average CPU usage research groups might expect with their Default RAP on each cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Research groups may be able to use, on average, up to 200 core years on each cluster with their Default RAP.

qa_pairs.question: Is the 200 core years CPU target a strict reservation or cap?
qa_pairs.answer: No, 200 core years is a variable target; it is not a reservation nor a cap.

qa_pairs.question: Can research groups use more or less than 200 core years of CPU resources?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, research groups could utilize more or less than that amount depending on the shape and size of the jobs and on the overall utilization of the cluster.

qa_pairs.question: How do most research groups meet their CPU resource needs?
qa_pairs.answer: Most research groups are able to meet their need for CPU resources by submitting jobs opportunistically with their Default RAP and without having to apply for the RAC.

qa_pairs.question: When might applying for a RAC be the best option for CPU resources?
qa_pairs.answer: Applying for RAC may be the best option if jobs have large memory requirements that take longer to run, and the amount of CPU resources needed exceeds the minimum required to apply.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find more information about job scheduling and CPU usage charges?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can find more information by reading about Allocations and compute scheduling.

qa_pairs.question: Are GPU resources available for opportunistic use?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, GPU resources are available for opportunistic use to all research groups with an active Alliance account.

qa_pairs.question: What is the trend for GPU demand?
qa_pairs.answer: The demand for GPUs is increasing quickly with the advances in Artificial Intelligence.

qa_pairs.question: How does the availability of GPU resources fluctuate?
qa_pairs.answer: The availability of GPU resources varies greatly over the year, becoming more limited in periods preceding major conferences.

qa_pairs.question: Can a specific amount of GPU resources be guaranteed for opportunistic use?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the Alliance cannot guarantee any amount of resources available to each group for opportunistic use, especially during times of high demand.

qa_pairs.question: Who can consistently use their allocated GPU amount?
qa_pairs.answer: Users with a RAC award should be able to consistently use their allocated amount.

qa_pairs.question: When can modest amounts of cloud resources be requested?
qa_pairs.answer: Modest amounts of cloud resources can be requested at any time.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find details about the limits for cloud RAS allocations?
qa_pairs.answer: Details about the limits for cloud RAS allocations are found in the cloud RAS documentation.

qa_pairs.question: Who must complete the form when requesting cloud resources?
qa_pairs.answer: PIs must complete the specified form with details about the cloud resources needed.

qa_pairs.question: What is the link to the form for requesting cloud resources?
qa_pairs.answer: The form for requesting cloud resources is located at: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeU_BoRk5cEz3AvVLf3e9yZJq-OvcFCQ-mg7p4AWXmUkd5rTw/viewform.

qa_pairs.question: Can users other than PIs be granted access to cloud resources?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, other users with an Alliance account may also be granted access to these resources.

qa_pairs.question: Who should be contacted for questions or help regarding RAS cloud resources?
qa_pairs.answer: For questions or help, users should contact cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca.
